methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Mara is a seed-stage defense and security company founded by Daniel Kofman. Valuation analysis relies on a comparables set of four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage peers in the Defense & Security space, comprising one seed-stage company (Tenna Systems at $60M) and three Series A companies (Ares Industries at $150M, Vatn Systems at $300M, and Stark Defence at $500M). The broader comp universe includes two public companies and eight additional private firms across frontier defense.

The peer-median method applies here as the primary triangulation approach. The median valuation across the four-company peer set is $225M, with a range spanning $60M (low) to $500M (high). This median-based implied valuation of $225M reflects the central tendency of companies operating in similar technology and financing environments.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $191M to $259M, centered on $225M. This relatively wide band—approximately ±15% around the median—reflects stage heterogeneity within the peer set: one seed-stage comparable anchors the low end, while three Series A companies pull the distribution higher. The primary uncertainty stems from limited data density at true seed stage; only one peer (Tenna Systems) directly matches Mara's stage, suggesting that early-stage capital market conditions and product maturity divergence may materially affect appropriate valuation within this range.
